# Labor sheet templates

Labor sheet templates work like customer quote templates but for the install version of the document. You set the header and footer once and pick the template when you generate a labor sheet for a quote.

## When to use them

- You have different installer crews with different info on their sheets.
- You want consistent site-info layout across all your jobs.
- You want a default that gets used on every labor sheet automatically.

## What's in the template

- Header - site info layout, your company name and contact, fixer contact lines.
- Footer - install notes, safety reminders, scope statements.
- Quote number / job reference layout.

The line items and labor amounts come from the quote itself.

## Steps to create one

1. Go to **Templates** in the workspace nav. Find the labor sheet templates section.
2. Click **New Labor Sheet Template**.
3. Add a name.
4. Build the header and footer text.
5. Save.

## Steps to use one

1. From a quote summary, click **Labor Sheet**.
2. In the labor sheet editor, pick your template from the dropdown.
3. Save.

## Tips

- Use the footer for safety reminders and scope notes. Fixers actually read footers when they're standing on site.
- If your installers are different on different jobs, you might want one template per crew.
